2020-10-01.title: Layoffs grow as companies gauge the course of the pandemic — and prospects for federal aid.
2020-10-01.abstract: American employers continue to cut jobs, signaling new anxiety about the course of the coronavirus pandemic and uncertainty about further legislative relief.

2020-10-01.title: ‘I feel like crying’: In Rio, the pandemic has stopped an unstoppable parade.
2020-10-01.abstract: Wars, disease and political turmoil have never prevented Rio de Janeiro from putting on its famous carnival. Now, the pandemic has forced a suspension of the annual parade for the first time since 1932.

2020-10-01.title: Russian Opposition Leader Navalny Says Putin Is Behind His Poisoning
2020-10-01.abstract: In an interview with Der Spiegel, Aleksei Navalny said the use of a military-grade nerve agent was proof that Russia’s president was involved.

2020-10-01.title: Jacinda Ardern Admits Past Cannabis Use. New Zealanders Shrug: ‘Us, Too.’
2020-10-01.abstract: As a former marijuana smoker, the prime minister has plenty of company in New Zealand. But she stopped short of backing legalization in a referendum this month.

2020-10-01.title: Fans celebrate the Stanley Cup champions, well outside the N.H.L.’s pandemic bubble.
2020-10-01.abstract: The Tampa Bay Lightning won the National Hockey League’s top trophy on Monday from within the confines of a 24/7 pandemic bubble in western Canada, and without any fans in the arena to watch.

2020-10-02.title: A top Trump aide has tested positive for the virus.
2020-10-02.abstract: One of President Trump’s most senior advisers has tested positive for the coronavirus, three people familiar with the matter said Thursday night.

2020-10-02.title: 30 Years After Reunification, Old German-German Border Is a Green Oasis
2020-10-02.abstract: Crossing the militarized border that split Germany into east and west once meant risking death. Now? It’s a literal walk in the park.

2020-10-02.title: Indonesia’s Stimulus Plan Draws Fire From Environmentalists and Unions
2020-10-02.abstract: Critics say the sweeping measure near passage in Parliament would lead to greater deforestation and the loss of worker protections.

2020-10-02.title: Covid-19 Upends South Korea’s Thanksgiving, and Its Rituals
2020-10-02.abstract: Millions have canceled family gatherings during this year’s Chuseok harvest festival at the government’s request. The anxiety is palpable.

2020-10-02.title: Trump and first lady test positive for the virus.
2020-10-02.abstract: President Trump said early Friday that he and his wife, Melania, had tested positive for the coronavirus, throwing the nation’s leadership into uncertainty and escalating the crisis posed by a pandemic that has already killed more than 207,000  Americans and devastated the economy.

2020-10-03.title: After 30 Years, East and West Germans Wonder: How United Are We?
2020-10-03.abstract: With Germany now an established, reunified democracy, it is taking stock of what is working and what still needs work, especially as the economy continues to lag in the former East.

2020-10-03.title: Germany’s Far Right Reunified, Too, Making It Much Stronger
2020-10-03.abstract: Thirty years after Germany came back together, the former East has become the stronghold of a once-marginalized movement that now sits in Parliament.

2020-10-03.title: Trump says he feels better, but his chief of staff says he is ‘still not on a clear path to a full recovery.’
2020-10-03.abstract: President Trump’s vital signs were “very concerning” over the last day and he is not out of danger, the White House chief of staff said on Saturday, contradicting a rosier picture painted by the president’s doctors on television just minutes before.

2020-10-03.title: India’s Covid-19 Death Toll Passes 100,000
2020-10-03.abstract: Only the United States and Brazil have reported more deaths from the coronavirus. Despite its climbing numbers, India plans to lift more restrictions.

2020-10-03.title: Contact Tracing, Key to Reining In the Virus, Falls Flat in the West
2020-10-03.abstract: Tracing is seen as a vital tool to avoid lockdowns and open economies, but that requires a robust system, widespread rapid testing and public trust. All are lacking in the West.

2020-10-04.title: To ward off pessimism about his health, Trump releases a video from the hospital.
2020-10-04.abstract: On Saturday, President Trump released a four-minute video from Walter Reed National Military Medical Center in Bethesda, Md., where he is receiving inpatient care for the coronavirus, to say that he is “starting to feel good.”

2020-10-04.title: Mike Pompeo cuts visits to South Korea and Mongolia from his Asia trip.
2020-10-04.abstract: As President Trump remains hospitalized with Covid-19, Secretary of State Mike Pompeo will cut short a trip to Asia this week, canceling stops in South Korea and Mongolia but continuing with a visit to Japan.

2020-10-04.title: A Black Belgian Student Saw a White Fraternity as His Ticket. It Was His Death.
2020-10-04.abstract: Sanda Dia’s death after an initiation ritual was regarded as a tragic accident. Newly released videos and photos have made it a symbol of growing intolerance.

2020-10-04.title: Hopes Fade for New Political Course in Algeria a Year After Popular Uprising
2020-10-04.abstract: “We are moving backward fast,” said a leading figure in protests last year that ousted the country’s authoritarian ruler.

2020-10-04.title: President Trump, undergoing an experimental antiviral treatment, spends a third day in the hospital.
2020-10-04.abstract: A barrage of conflicting accounts created confusion about his medical condition. Mr. Trump released a video from Walter Reed National Military Medical Center in Bethesda, Md., projecting optimism.

2020-10-05.title: Interpol Issues ‘Red Notice’ for Thai Red Bull Heir
2020-10-05.abstract: The police in Thailand, widely criticized for dropping charges against the wealthy playboy in July after he was accused of a fatal hit-and-run, are seeking the agency’s help to arrest him.

2020-10-05.title: The White House outbreak grows as Kayleigh McEnany, the press secretary, tests positive for the virus.
2020-10-05.abstract: Some of the treatments President Trump is receiving would normally be for a more severe case than the White House has disclosed, outside experts say.

2020-10-05.title: At Afghan Peace Talks, Hoping to End Their Fathers’ War
2020-10-05.abstract: The children of the war against the Soviets carry legacies of loss and determination — and their own generation’s crimes — as they meet to try to break a cycle of devastation.

2020-10-05.title: In Canada, Kamala Harris, a Disco-Dancing Teenager, Yearned for Home
2020-10-05.abstract: Senator Harris spent her adolescent years in Canada. Friends say being a California girl at a multicultural public high school in Montreal helped shape her.

2020-10-05.title: Paris will close its bars and many cafes for at least two weeks starting on Tuesday.
2020-10-05.abstract: Bars in Paris will close for two weeks starting on Tuesday, the authorities there announced on Monday, as France tries to stem a surge of coronavirus cases in its capital. The measure will also affect most cafes, which in many cases serve alcohol, but little or no food. Restaurants will be able to remain open if they follow a strict health protocol.

2020-10-06.title: Contact tracing efforts are restricted to the two days before Trump’s diagnosis.
2020-10-06.abstract: Despite almost daily disclosures of new coronavirus infections among President Trump’s close associates, the White House is making little effort to investigate the scope and source of its outbreak.

2020-10-06.title: Cuomo won’t approve closing businesses in 9 N.Y.C. hot spots, but will close schools in those areas on Tuesday.
2020-10-06.abstract: Gov. Andrew M. Cuomo of New York refused on Monday to allow New York City to close nonessential businesses in nine hot spots in Brooklyn and Queens where the coronavirus has spiked, pre-empting a plan announced the day before by Mayor Bill de Blasio.

2020-10-06.title: Kyrgyzstan in Chaos After Protesters Seize Government Buildings
2020-10-06.abstract: The country’s electoral agency annulled the results of Sunday’s disputed parliamentary elections and the president offered to mediate. But it remained unclear who was in control.

2020-10-06.title: Pompeo’s Message in Japan: Countering China Is Worth Meeting Face to Face
2020-10-06.abstract: The American diplomat’s willingness to meet with allies in Asia, despite the crisis embroiling the White House, speaks to fears of China’s rise.

2020-10-06.title: Trump says he is pulling the plug on stimulus talks.
2020-10-06.abstract: President Trump on Tuesday abruptly ended talks with Democrats on an economic stimulus bill, sending the stock market sliding and dealing a final blow to an intensive set of on-again-off-again negotiations to deliver additional pandemic aid to struggling Americans before the November elections.

2020-10-07.title: North Korean Diplomat, Missing Since 2018, Is in Seoul, Lawmaker Says
2020-10-07.abstract: Jo Song-gil was the North’s acting ambassador to Rome when he vanished, raising the possibility that he had become the highest-ranking official to defect to the South since 1997.

2020-10-07.title: Hidden Beneath the Ocean’s Surface, Nearly 16 Million Tons of Microplastic
2020-10-07.abstract: New research shows that the amount of fragments embedded in the sea floor is far more than the plastic floating on the ocean’s surface.

2020-10-07.title: Venezuela, Once an Oil Giant, Reaches the End of an Era
2020-10-07.abstract: Venezuela’s oil reserves, the world’s largest, transformed the country and the global energy market. Now its oil sector is grinding to a halt. Will it ever recover?

2020-10-07.title: Golden Dawn Found Guilty of Running Criminal Organization in Greece
2020-10-07.abstract: The court tied the far-right party to a string of attacks, including the fatal stabbing of a left-wing rapper.

2020-10-07.title: Hurricane Delta Moves Into Gulf After Striking, but Mostly Sparing, Mexico
2020-10-07.abstract: The Category 2 storm passed over the Yucatán Peninsula and was expected to reach the Gulf Coast of the United States by Friday. No deaths were reported in Mexico.

2020-10-08.title: How do you lose an ‘S.N.L.’ gig? Ask Morgan Wallen.
2020-10-08.abstract: Morgan Wallen, a country musician who was scheduled to perform on “Saturday Night Live” this weekend, won’t be appearing on the show after all, citing its coronavirus protocols.

2020-10-08.title: Nevada halts use of rapid coronavirus tests in nursing homes, citing inaccuracies.
2020-10-08.abstract: The coronavirus tests kits are small and fast — they produce results in as a little as 15 minutes — and when they were first distributed to nursing homes around the country in August by the federal government, they were welcomed with open arms.

2020-10-08.title: Kamala Harris calls Trump’s virus response the ‘greatest failure of any presidential administration.’
2020-10-08.abstract: At the vice-presidential debate on Wednesday night, Senator Kamala Harris of California wasted no time in prosecuting the case against the Trump administration’s handling of the coronavirus crisis.

2020-10-08.title: The virus dominates the vice-presidential debate between Pence and Harris.
2020-10-08.abstract: Vice President Mike Pence and Senator Kamala Harris clashed over the Trump administration’s handling of the coronavirus pandemic on Wednesday night, with Mr. Pence defending the White House’s record without addressing its fundamental failures, while Ms. Harris accused him and President Trump of presiding over a catastrophic failure in public-health policy.

2020-10-08.title: A wave of death in Sweden’s nursing homes has exposed holes in a famously generous safety net.
2020-10-08.abstract: In the popular imagination, Sweden does not seem like the sort of country prone to accepting the mass death of grandparents to conserve resources in a pandemic.

2020-10-09.title: Australia, Tell Us the Places in the World You Love the Most
2020-10-09.abstract: For this year’s 52 Places feature, we’re looking for reader submissions.

2020-10-09.title: An Afghan Boy’s Rape and Death Prompt a Rare Response: Arrests
2020-10-09.abstract: Villagers confronted officials, and seven police officers are now in jail, in a rare official action to investigate child sexual abuse accusations in Afghanistan.

2020-10-09.title: ‘Eventually, slowly, it comes back’: The U.S. Northeast sees signs of a second wave.
2020-10-09.abstract: The Northeastern United States, devastated by the coronavirus in the spring and then held up as a model of infection control by the summer, is now seeing the first inklings of what might become a second wave of the virus.

2020-10-09.title: World Food Program Awarded Nobel Peace Prize for Work During Pandemic
2020-10-09.abstract: The Nobel committee said the U.N. agency’s work to address hunger had laid the foundations for peace in nations ravaged by war.

2020-10-09.title: What if Local and Diverse Is Better Than Networked and Global?
2020-10-09.abstract: Helena Norberg-Hodge has been arguing for localism since the 1970s, but the pandemic is making the Australian activist-scholar’s ideas more relevant than ever.

2020-10-10.title: Hong Kong Arrests Nine Over Protesters’ Escape by Speedboat
2020-10-10.abstract: The arrests appeared intended to deter others from helping antigovernment protesters flee the Chinese territory, where Beijing’s grip is tightening.

2020-10-10.title: A Famed Horror Director Mines Japan’s Real-Life Atrocities
2020-10-10.abstract: Kiyoshi Kurosawa’s latest movie, which recently won him the award for best director at the Venice Film Festival, is a thriller animated by the ghosts of Japan’s ghastly wartime experiments.

2020-10-10.title: For Boris Johnson, and Maybe Trump, Covid as Metaphor Is Hard to Shake
2020-10-10.abstract: The British prime minister’s personal battle with the disease has come to symbolize his government’s fumbling efforts to halt the pathogen.

2020-10-10.title: North Korea Unveils What Appears to Be New ICBM During Military Parade
2020-10-10.abstract: The weapon appeared to be bigger than the North’s previous long-range missile, indicating that it might be able to fly farther with a more powerful nuclear warhead. But it is untested.

2020-10-10.title: Fighting Eases, Briefly, After Cease-Fire Between Armenia and Azerbaijan
2020-10-10.abstract: The Armenian Defense Ministry said most of the front line was “relatively calm.” But renewed shelling was reported at night.

2020-10-11.title: QAnon Is Thriving in Germany. The Extreme Right Is Delighted.
2020-10-11.abstract: As the U.S. conspiracy theory goes global, it has found fertile ground in the putsch fantasies and anti-Semitic tropes long popular on Germany’s far-right fringe. Counterterrorism officials worry.

2020-10-11.title: In Belarus, Women Led the Protests and Shattered Stereotypes
2020-10-11.abstract: The women who organized the political campaign and the subsequent protests against President Aleksandr G. Lukashenko may ultimately be defeated. But society may never be the same.

2020-10-11.title: With Elections Ahead, Some African Presidents Try Engineering Results
2020-10-11.abstract: Presidential elections are scheduled soon in at least 10 African countries. Many incumbents are changing constitutions and bending rules to ensure they stay in power.

2020-10-11.title: Offering few details, the White House doctor says Trump is no longer contagious.
2020-10-11.abstract: President Trump’s doctor said in a memo released Saturday night that he was “no longer considered a transmission risk to others,” as the president prepared to resume campaign activities this week.

2020-10-11.title: ‘Call Me a Dreamer’: A Shattered Beirut Neighborhood Rebuilds
2020-10-11.abstract: Many worry that a full recovery won’t be possible, but residents of one of Beirut’s most diverse and cosmopolitan areas are moving back in and trying to repair the damage from the August explosion.

2020-10-12.title: Regeneron’s C.E.O. says its promising Covid-19 treatment must be rationed.
2020-10-12.abstract: President Trump said over the weekend that the experimental medication he was given to fight his Covid-19 infection was “standard, pretty routine.” It seems likely it will be anything but should it ever reach the market.

2020-10-12.title: In Former Taliban Stronghold, Defiant Women Hit the Gym
2020-10-12.abstract: A women’s health club in Kandahar challenges conservative Afghan traditions that vilify exercise for women. Many work out secretly.

2020-10-12.title: The French prime minister warns that local lockdowns may be imposed as cases surge.
2020-10-12.abstract: France is weighing the possibility of local lockdowns as the country battles a second wave of the coronavirus epidemic, Prime Minister Jean Castex said Monday.

2020-10-12.title: Bangladesh to Allow Death Penalty for Rape Convictions
2020-10-12.abstract: Ministers approved an amendment that would permit capital punishment for the crime after a series of assaults led to widespread calls to tackle what many say is a systemic problem.

2020-10-12.title: Taliban Test Afghan and U.S. Resolve in Talks by Attacking a City
2020-10-12.abstract: The insurgents have opened an offensive against the capital of Helmand Province even as their negotiators remain at the table in Qatar.

2020-10-13.title: At a campaign rally, Trump offers to give ‘a big fat kiss’ to attendees.
2020-10-13.abstract: President Trump, eager to prove he is healthy and energetic despite his recent hospitalization for Covid-19, returned to the campaign trail on Monday night in Florida, telling supporters he was fully recovered and therefore immune to the coronavirus — a claim for which there is no conclusive scientific backing.

2020-10-13.title: Johnson & Johnson pauses its coronavirus vaccine trial because of a volunteer’s ‘unexplained illness.’
2020-10-13.abstract: Johnson & Johnson has paused the large late-stage clinical trial of its coronavirus vaccine because of an “unexplained illness” in one of the volunteers, the company said on Monday.

2020-10-13.title: July is the new January: More companies delay a return to the office.
2020-10-13.abstract: When the coronavirus pandemic closed offices around the United States in March, many companies told their employees that it would be a short hiatus.

2020-10-13.title: Iceland’s tourism industry is preparing for a comeback.
2020-10-13.abstract: Tourism is undergoing a downturn all over the world, but several factors make Iceland particularly vulnerable to the industry’s crash: geographic isolation, a small domestic population, strict border measures and an economy that — after an extraordinary, decade-long tourism boom — had come to depend heavily on foreign tourists. A recent surge in coronavirus cases has added to Iceland’s challenges.

2020-10-13.title: As Trump flouts safety protocols, news outlets balk at close coverage.
2020-10-13.abstract: Major news organizations have become increasingly wary of sending journalists to travel with President Trump to White House events and campaign rallies, as the president and his aides continue to shun safety protocols after an outbreak of the virus within their ranks.

2020-10-14.title: He Built a Trump Statue and Worshiped It. Then He Collapsed.
2020-10-14.abstract: Bussa Krishna, a farmer who called President Trump his god, stopped eating after Mr. Trump became infected with the coronavirus, his family said. He died on Sunday.

2020-10-14.title: For Indian Women Working as Cooks and Nannies, No #MeToo Moment
2020-10-14.abstract: A new study by Human Rights Watch found that poor Indian women who work in the informal jobs sector are routinely subjected to sexual harassment and abuse despite a groundbreaking law.

2020-10-14.title: ‘This Is a War’: Cross-Border Fight Over Water Erupts in Mexico
2020-10-14.abstract: Farmers in Mexico ambushed soldiers and seized a dam to stop water payments to the United States, in a sign of growing conflict over increasingly scarce resources.

2020-10-14.title: Court Sentences Leaders of Greece’s Golden Dawn to Prison
2020-10-14.abstract: The neo-fascist party’s leaders were convicted of running a criminal organization, and some of its members were found guilty of acts of violence.

2020-10-14.title: E.U. Preparing Sanctions on Putin Allies Over Navalny’s Poisoning
2020-10-14.abstract: Six high-ranking Russian officials who are believed to be involved in the attack on the opposition leader Aleksei A. Navalny face asset freezes and bans on travel to the bloc.

2020-10-15.title: The Jailed Activist Left a Letter Behind. The Message: Keep Fighting.
2020-10-15.abstract: Pham Doan Trang was harassed for years by the Vietnamese police, and beaten. In a letter, she foretold her arrest and called for an end to one-party rule.

2020-10-15.title: As the virus spread, private briefings from the Trump administration fueled a stock sell-off.
2020-10-15.abstract: On the afternoon of Feb. 24, President Trump declared on Twitter that the coronavirus was “very much under control” in the United States, but hours earlier, senior members of the president’s economic team, privately addressing board members of the conservative Hoover Institution, were less confident.

2020-10-15.title: This Urban Safari Comes With a Warning: Watch Out for Snakes
2020-10-15.abstract: On nighttime hikes in Hong Kong’s surprisingly lush forests, a snake catcher teaches city slickers about the reptiles — and their bites.

2020-10-15.title: Singapore and Hong Kong agree to set up a travel bubble, and other news from around the world.
2020-10-15.abstract: Officials in Singapore and Hong Kong said Thursday that they had reached a preliminary agreement to establish a travel bubble between the two Asian financial centers, allowing travelers of all kinds to bypass quarantine.

2020-10-15.title: From Hiding, Head of Central Asia’s Only Democracy Says He’s Quitting
2020-10-15.abstract: The president of Kyrgyzstan said he did not want to be remembered as a leader “who shed blood and shot at his own citizens.”

2020-10-16.title: Will Jacinda Ardern Win a Second Term? New Zealand’s Election, Explained
2020-10-16.abstract: Voters go to the polls on Saturday after a campaign that focused heavily on Ms. Ardern’s successful handling of the coronavirus outbreak.

2020-10-16.title: In One of the World’s Longest Lockdowns, One Man Is Omnipresent
2020-10-16.abstract: Daniel Andrews, Victoria’s premier, has conducted 100 consecutive daily news conferences. In Melbourne, we all watch. We are all exhausted.

2020-10-16.title: Trump supports a bigger relief bill, but a deal is far from certain.
2020-10-16.abstract: President Trump, struggling to gain traction among voters just weeks before the election, called on Thursday for a bigger stimulus package than he had previously offered, and the White House signaled it was willing to make concessions to Democrats. But the proposals were unlikely to win the necessary backing from Senate Republicans who are preparing a far smaller bill of their own.

2020-10-16.title: Faced with twin crises, Britain’s leader is stalling for time.
2020-10-16.abstract: For Prime Minister Boris Johnson of Britain, the pandemic and the looming domestic upheaval it has overshadowed — Brexit — are linked.

2020-10-16.title: Trump and Biden offer starkly different views of the pandemic during dueling events.
2020-10-16.abstract: In one of his final prime-time television events before Election Day, President Trump, who is trailing in national and battleground polls, offered little new to voters who may still be undecided, staking out positions on the coronavirus that are at odds with both the scientific consensus and public opinion.

2020-10-17.title: Will we remember how hugging works when we are allowed to do it again?
2020-10-17.abstract: As the weeks of coronavirus quarantine stretched into months, hugs are among the many things isolated people found themselves aching for. Hugs are good for humans — perhaps more valuable than many of us realized, until we found ourselves missing them.

2020-10-17.title: Jacinda Ardern, Hero to Liberals Abroad, Is Validated at Home
2020-10-17.abstract: New Zealand’s prime minister and her party are coasting to victory in national elections, though it is unclear how far she will push her progressive promises.

2020-10-17.title: For Young Rohingya Brides, Marriage Means a Perilous, Deadly Crossing
2020-10-17.abstract: Girls and young women from refugee camps in Bangladesh, promised to men they have never met, are undertaking the dangerous journey to Malaysia to join them.

2020-10-17.title: Suspect Stalked French School Before Beheading Teacher, Officials Say
2020-10-17.abstract: The suspect, a Russian immigrant, was angered by a classroom display of caricatures of the Prophet Muhammad and sought help identifying the teacher involved.

2020-10-17.title: Bill Offering L.G.B.T. Protections in Italy Spurs Rallies on Both Sides
2020-10-17.abstract: Supporters frame the measure as a long-overdue means to provide basic human rights. Opponents depict it as an overreaching step that would suppress opinion.

2020-10-18.title: In Election, Bolivia Confronts Legacy of Ousted Socialist Leader
2020-10-18.abstract: Sunday’s election is seen as a referendum on Evo Morales, who lifted many out of poverty in his 14 years as president, but whose policies and tone divided the country.

2020-10-18.title: Trump’s Sanctions on International Court May Do Little Beyond Alienating Allies
2020-10-18.abstract: Critics say the administration has targeted a human rights lawyer with economic penalties meant for warlords, dictators and authoritarian governments.

2020-10-18.title: ‘It’s insane’: This is what happened when schools reopened in a virus-hit community.
2020-10-18.abstract: In mid-September, parents of students at Corner Canyon High School, in an affluent suburb of Salt Lake City, received a text urging them to beg the school board to keep classrooms open.

2020-10-18.title: Pandemic weariness is making it harder to stem outbreaks, health officials say.
2020-10-18.abstract: In parts of the world where the coronavirus is resurging, the outbreaks and a rising sense of apathy are colliding, making for a dangerous combination.

2020-10-18.title: A Storied Female Warlord Surrenders, Taliban Say, Exposing Afghan Weakness
2020-10-18.abstract: She was undefeated in decades of war, but her decision was an act of survival. A government that has come to depend on unreliable militias finds itself vulnerable.

2020-10-19.title: With Covid-19 under control, China’s economy surges ahead.
2020-10-19.abstract: As most of the world still struggles with the pandemic, China is showing once again that a fast economic rebound is possible when the virus is brought under control.

2020-10-19.title: An Earthquake, an Orphanage, and New Beginnings for Haitian Children in America
2020-10-19.abstract: After the 2010 earthquake in Haiti, 19 children from one orphanage were flown to the U.S. to be adopted by American families. One would later meet President Trump.

2020-10-19.title: ‘If There Were No Hashish Here, You Wouldn’t See a Single House’
2020-10-19.abstract: A village in Lebanon, where cannabis grows everywhere, has long counted on hashish for income. But the country’s economic crisis has farmers reconsidering the crop.

2020-10-19.title: Landslide in Vietnam Kills at Least 20 Military Personnel
2020-10-19.abstract: Search-and-rescue efforts were underway after a landslide in the central province of Quang Tri, resulting in what may be the country’s greatest military loss in peacetime.

2020-10-19.title: 2,000-Year-Old Cat Etching Found at Nazca Lines Site in Peru
2020-10-19.abstract: Archaeologists came across the faded feline outline while conducting maintenance work at the UNESCO heritage site.

2020-10-20.title: 1,300 Prisoners Escape From Congo Jail After an Attack Claimed by ISIS
2020-10-20.abstract: Local officials blamed an Islamist militant group for the assault on the prison and a nearby military camp in the northeastern part of the country.

2020-10-20.title: As Britain edges toward lockdowns, long-held grievances flare up.
2020-10-20.abstract: Britain’s desperate attempt to curb a second wave of the coronavirus has exposed multiple fractures in its politics and society, but perhaps none as jagged as that between Prime Minister Boris Johnson and the mayor of Greater Manchester, the country’s second-largest urban area.

2020-10-20.title: 1945: Strike Paralyzes Argentina
2020-10-20.abstract: A 24-hour walkout backed by the government to show support for Col. Juan D. Peron brought the country to a standstill.

2020-10-20.title: Mask mandate? In a Montana town, it ‘puts us at odds with customers.’
2020-10-20.abstract: This summer, Governor Steve Bullock of Montana mandated face coverings in public spaces to combat a spike in Covid-19 cases. But the sheriff in the town of Hamilton, backed up by the Ravalli County commissioners, elected not to enforce the order, saying individual rights took priority.

2020-10-20.title: Line holders are paid to wait all night outside a motor vehicle office.
2020-10-20.abstract: In a New Jersey suburb, a woman sets up a tent around 8 p.m. outside a state Motor Vehicle Commission office. She will spend the night there, accompanied by her 4-year-old son, until just before the office opens at 8 a.m. The woman, Sumbal Nadeem, doesn’t need a driver’s license or anything else from the agency.

2020-10-21.title: India Returns a Chinese Soldier Who Got Lost Looking for Yaks
2020-10-21.abstract: After months of tension that turned violent at times, the release signaled a respite in a dispute that has led to a buildup of forces along the disputed mountainous border.

2020-10-21.title: ‘We Cannot Kill That Little Chihuahua’: Actor’s Puppy Crusade Shines Light on Global Trade
2020-10-21.abstract: The action star Jean-Claude Van Damme intervened to save a Bulgarian dog from euthanasia after Norway said it had been imported on a fake passport.

2020-10-21.title: 2 Broke Artists Started a Bakery at Home. It’s a Pandemic Hit.
2020-10-21.abstract: They didn’t have an oven. Their apartment resembled Santa’s workshop set up in a dorm room. But two rookie bakers are thriving in Mexico City, where food and entrepreneurism go hand-in-hand.

2020-10-21.title: Demand for Pakistan Visas Sets Off Deadly Stampede in Afghanistan
2020-10-21.abstract: At least 12 people died as thousands crowded a soccer stadium. Many were seeking medical care in Pakistan, which recently eased its pandemic border restrictions.

2020-10-21.title: Epsilon Is Now a Category 3 Hurricane
2020-10-21.abstract: The storm is the 10th hurricane of the season. A tropical storm warning is in effect for Bermuda.

2020-10-22.title: Europe Wonders if It Can Rely on U.S. Again, Whoever Wins
2020-10-22.abstract: America’s deeply polarized politics have marked foreign policy, too, undermining Washington’s authority and reputation for reliability.

2020-10-22.title: Forgotten on the presidential campaign: schools in crisis.
2020-10-22.abstract: For months now, the extraordinary challenges of schooling during the coronavirus pandemic have dominated life in communities large and small across the United States, yet the topic has not been at the forefront of the presidential campaign.

2020-10-22.title: Trump in North Carolina: ‘All you hear is Covid, Covid, Covid, Covid, Covid.’
2020-10-22.abstract: President Trump complained about the news media’s intensive coverage of the coronavirus pandemic at a campaign rally in Gastonia, N.C., on Wednesday evening, describing the disease as an annoying inconvenience even as the country’s case count and death toll continue to soar.

2020-10-22.title: ‘These are everyday people who are dying’: A study offers a new picture of the lives lost to the virus.
2020-10-22.abstract: There have been more than 220,000 coronavirus deaths in the United States, and each one represents years of potential life lost.

2020-10-22.title: OSHA is under fire over its regulation of meatpacking plants.
2020-10-22.abstract: When the pandemic hit in March, a JBS meatpacking plant in Greeley, Colo., began providing paid leave to workers at high risk of serious illness.

2020-10-23.title: Pope Francis’ Views on Same-Sex Civil Unions Were Cut From a 2019 Vatican Interview
2020-10-23.abstract: A Mexican broadcast company says Francis made the comments to its correspondent more than a year ago, and people close to the company say the Vatican then edited them out.

2020-10-23.title: Swimming to Sanity in Melbourne’s Lockdown
2020-10-23.abstract: As the city creeps out of one of the world’s longest lockdowns, the reopening of outdoor pools grants a partial freedom.

2020-10-23.title: E.U. Says Veggie Burgers Can Keep Their Name
2020-10-23.abstract: The European Parliament voted on Friday on proposals that would have banned products without meat from being labeled burgers or sausages.

2020-10-23.title: In a calmer debate, Trump and Biden offer sharply different visions of the pandemic.
2020-10-23.abstract: President Trump and Joseph R. Biden Jr. delivered starkly divergent closing arguments to the country in the final presidential debate on Thursday, offering opposite prognoses for a range of issues, including the coronavirus pandemic.

2020-10-23.title: For Italy’s Masked Rap Diva, an Era of Face Coverings Poses a Dilemma
2020-10-23.abstract: Myss Keta, the mysterious Queen of the Milan Night and an authority on life behind the surgical veil, was on the brink of celebrity. Now, everyone looks like her.

2020-10-24.title: Bangkok Is Engulfed by Protests. What’s Driving Them?
2020-10-24.abstract: Rebellious youths. A history of coups. A royal family protected by laws that can punish critics. All are key ingredients in the struggle convulsing Thailand.

2020-10-24.title: She Used to Clean City Hall. Now, She Runs It.
2020-10-24.abstract: In Russia, elections are typically theatrical affairs with the winner and loser preordained. Sometimes, though, they go off script.

2020-10-24.title: New Airport, Beleaguered Symbol of ‘Irreverent’ Berlin, Is Opening at Long Last
2020-10-24.abstract: Nine years late and $4 billion over budget, the airport is already outdated. Repeated blunders dented the image of German efficiency, but the “poor but sexy” capital has long been a bit different.

2020-10-24.title: President Andrzej Duda of Poland Tests Positive for Coronavirus
2020-10-24.abstract: A spokesman said the head of state was feeling well despite a positive test result. A second political leader is also in isolation after he came in contact with an infected person.

2020-10-24.title: Indigenous Colombians, Facing New Wave of Brutality, Demand Government Action
2020-10-24.abstract: “If we don’t stand before the world and say, ‘This is happening,’ we will be exterminated,” a protest leader said. After a long civil war, a new type of violence is sweeping Indigenous communities.

2020-10-25.title: Pence’s chief of staff and several other aides test positive for the virus.
2020-10-25.abstract: At least three top aides to Vice President Mike Pence have tested positive for the coronavirus in the last few days, people briefed on the matter said. The test results raise fresh questions about the safety protocols at the White House, where masks are not routinely worn.

2020-10-25.title: With Vacation Rentals Empty, European Cities See a Chance to Reclaim Housing
2020-10-25.abstract: Platforms like Airbnb have been criticized for raising living costs in popular cities. Now, officials are trying to move properties aimed at tourists to long-term leases.

2020-10-25.title: Pope Francis Appoints First African-American Cardinal
2020-10-25.abstract: Wilton Gregory, the archbishop of Washington, was among 13 new cardinals named on Sunday.

2020-10-25.title: In coronavirus testing, false positives are more hazardous than they might seem.
2020-10-25.abstract: In the high-stakes world of coronavirus testing, false positives are widely considered to be benign in comparison with false negatives, which can deprive infected people of treatment and embolden them to mingle with others, hastening the spread of disease.

2020-10-25.title: After making a comeback during the pandemic, pro sports face a tough second act.
2020-10-25.abstract: In mid-March, as the pandemic forced extensive lockdowns throughout the United States, professional sports came to a halt, with the very real possibility that no champions would be crowned in basketball, hockey or baseball.

2020-10-26.title: The Fox News president and several anchors were advised to quarantine after possible virus exposure.
2020-10-26.abstract: The president of Fox News and several anchors are quarantining after a positive virus test at the network.

2020-10-26.title: Women on Qatar Airways Flight Are Strip-Searched, Sparking Outrage in Australia
2020-10-26.abstract: Female passengers said they were pulled from a Sydney-bound flight in Doha and given a medically invasive exam to see if they had recently given birth after an abandoned newborn was found in an airport bathroom.

2020-10-26.title: A Teacher, His Killer and the Failure of French Integration
2020-10-26.abstract: For generations, public schools assimilated immigrant children into French society by instilling the nation’s ideals. The beheading of a teacher has raised doubts about whether that model still works.

2020-10-26.title: China Aims to End Extreme Poverty, but Covid-19 Exposes Gaps
2020-10-26.abstract: The pandemic has worsened longstanding conditions that have widened inequality, hindering Xi Jinping’s vow to “leave no one behind.”

2020-10-26.title: With a little more than a week until Election Day, Trump and Biden make their closing pitches on ‘60 Minutes.’
2020-10-26.abstract: Nine days before Election Day, President Trump and Joseph R. Biden Jr. offered sharply divergent visions for the country, including the coronavirus pandemic, in wide-ranging interviews on “60 Minutes.”

2020-10-27.title: How Trump and Bolsonaro Broke Latin America’s Covid-19 Defenses
2020-10-27.abstract: The two presidents drove out 10,000 Cuban doctors and nurses. They defunded the region’s leading health agency. They wrongly pushed hydroxychloroquine as a cure.

2020-10-27.title: Trump and Bolsonaro weakened Latin America’s health defenses at critical moments.
2020-10-27.abstract: The coronavirus was gathering lethal speed when President Trump met his Brazilian counterpart, Jair Bolsonaro, on March 7 for dinner at Mar-a-Lago. Mr. Bolsonaro had canceled trips that week to Italy, Poland and Hungary, and Brazil’s health minister had urged him to stay away from Florida, too.

2020-10-27.title: Eli Lilly said its antibody treatment does not work on patients hospitalized with Covid-19.
2020-10-27.abstract: The drug maker Eli Lilly said on Monday that its antibody treatment was ineffective on patients hospitalized with advanced Covid-19 and that a government-sponsored trial would not administer the drug to new participants.

2020-10-27.title: A nurse in a virus hot spot dies at 48.
2020-10-27.abstract: This obituary is part of a series about people who have died in the coronavirus pandemic. Read about others here.

2020-10-27.title: The White House holds another ceremony for Barrett, this time with more masks.
2020-10-27.abstract: President Trump on Monday held an unusual nighttime ceremony at the White House to swear in Justice Amy Coney Barrett to the Supreme Court, a month to the day after the superspreader event blamed for infecting multiple people with the coronavirus, including the president himself, who was forced to spend three nights in the hospital.

2020-10-28.title: Qatar Expresses ‘Regrets’ but Defends Invasive Exams of Women on 10 Flights
2020-10-28.abstract: Women were pulled from multiple flights and strip-searched, the Australian government said, after a newborn baby was found in an airport restroom.

2020-10-28.title: As Tanzania Votes, Many See Democracy Itself on the Ballot
2020-10-28.abstract: The Tanzanian president is nicknamed “the Bulldozer,” and he has brooked little dissent as he seeks a second term.

2020-10-28.title: Morale sags among British health workers, and other news from around the world.
2020-10-28.abstract: As Britain is hit by a second wave of coronavirus infections and deaths, the country’s doctors and nurses are bracing for what is expected to be a deluge of new patients over the next six months. But this time, they say, the wave is coming without the same sense of caution among a coronavirus-weary public, nor with a clear government strategy to contain the virus and address rapidly filling intensive care units.

2020-10-28.title: A player was pulled from Tuesday’s World Series game after testing positive.
2020-10-28.abstract: Moments after the Los Angeles Dodgers clinched the World Series title on Tuesday night, as their players and coaches mingled excitedly on the field before receiving their trophy, a Fox broadcaster delivered some shocking news: Justin Turner, the Dodgers’ longtime third baseman, had been taken out of the game because he had received a positive result on a coronavirus test.

2020-10-28.title: The Lockdown That Felt Like It Might Last Forever Has Finally Ended
2020-10-28.abstract: Melbourne, Australia’s second-largest city, emerged from its 111-day hibernation with a mix of euphoria and caution.

2020-10-29.title: M.L.B. says a Dodgers player insisted on celebrating with teammates despite his positive coronavirus test.
2020-10-29.abstract: The Los Angeles Dodgers’ joy over winning the World Series on Tuesday night was overshadowed when Justin Turner, the team’s veteran third baseman, joined his teammates in celebration on the field shortly after learning he had tested positive for the coronavirus.

2020-10-29.title: First a Sweet 16 party, now a wedding and a birthday: Long Island has more superspreading events.
2020-10-29.abstract: Health officials on Long Island were scrambling on Wednesday to respond to two coronavirus superspreader events that left 56 people with the virus and nearly 300 in quarantine after attending a wedding and a birthday party.

2020-10-29.title: Trump wrested control from doctors as the pandemic grew, Jared Kushner told an interviewer.
2020-10-29.abstract: As coronavirus cases and deaths were rising in the United States last spring, Jared Kushner, President Trump’s senior adviser and son-in-law, told an interviewer that the president had taken the “country back from the doctors.”

2020-10-29.title: Typhoon Molave Slams Into Vietnam, Bringing Death and More Misery
2020-10-29.abstract: Already battling devastating floods, the country was hit by one of its biggest storms in decades.

2020-10-29.title: Jobless young Europeans face desperate times as renewed virus restrictions loom.
2020-10-29.abstract: Like millions of young people across Europe, Rebecca Lee, 25, has suddenly found herself shut out of the labor market as the economic toll of the pandemic intensifies.

2020-10-30.title: New Zealand’s Advance Preview of a Post-Virus World
2020-10-30.abstract: What it feels like to leave New York and land in a parallel universe of calm politics and good health.

2020-10-30.title: As virus cases rise across the U.S., records topple.
2020-10-30.abstract: More than 2,000 new coronavirus cases in Colorado. More than 6,400 new cases in Illinois. And more than 1,000 new cases in New Mexico.

2020-10-30.title: An unproven Covid-19 drug is turning out to be a proven moneymaker for its manufacturer.
2020-10-30.abstract: The United States reached a milestone, of sorts, when last week the Food and Drug Administration approved the first treatment for Covid-19: Veklury, better known by its scientific name, remdesivir.

2020-10-30.title: Clemson’s star quarterback tests positive for the virus.
2020-10-30.abstract: Trevor Lawrence, the star quarterback at top-ranked Clemson and a leading contender for the Heisman Trophy, has tested positive for coronavirus, the university said Thursday night.

2020-10-30.title: New Zealand Voters Approve Euthanasia but Reject Recreational Marijuana
2020-10-30.abstract: Proponents of legalizing cannabis voiced anger at Prime Minister Jacinda Ardern, who revealed only after the referendum that she supported it.

2020-10-31.title: A Frazzled World Holds Its Breath While the U.S. Chooses Its Leader
2020-10-31.abstract: President Trump turned American foreign policy inside out, to the benefit of some nations and consternation of others. Now both groups are watching attentively to see which direction the U.S. goes next.

2020-10-31.title: Typhoon Goni Makes Landfall in Philippines, Packing ‘Catastrophic’ Winds
2020-10-31.abstract: “We are forecasting widespread destruction,” an official said of the typhoon, which made landfall early Sunday.

2020-10-31.title: Russian Provinces Hit by a Second Wave of Coronavirus
2020-10-31.abstract: Overall, Russia’s health care system is coping with a fall surge in cases, but bodies are piling up in overwhelmed regional hospitals and morgues.

2020-10-31.title: England to Shut Pubs, Restaurants and Most Shops as Virus Surges
2020-10-31.abstract: Prime Minister Boris Johnson’s cabinet proposed the move on Saturday as medical experts warned that the virus would soon overwhelm hospitals if the government did not take draconian action.

2020-10-31.title: As Tanzania’s President Wins a Second Term, Opposition Calls for Protests
2020-10-31.abstract: After a poll marred by accusations of widespread fraud and irregularities, one opposition leader warned that the nation could become a “one-party system.”
